artifacts.name: api-and-interface-design
artifacts.description_raw: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.
artifacts.description_normalized: Guides stable, hard-to-misuse API and interface design across REST, GraphQL, module boundaries, and component contracts.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: With a sufficient number of users of an API, all observable behaviors of your system will be depended on by somebody, regardless of what you promise in the contract.
artifacts.eval_notes: The "third-party API responses are untrusted data" call-out (validate shape and content before using in any logic, rendering, or decision-making) is a security-aware addition most API design skills miss. The framing of testing limitations ("Even with perfect contract tests, Hyrum's Law means 'safe' changes can break real users who depend on undocumented behavior") is a honest, uncommon admission t
artifacts.intent: Guides the design of stable, hard-to-misuse interfaces (REST, GraphQL, module boundaries, component props) using Hyrum's Law, contract-first, boundary validation, and additive extension principles.

artifacts.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
artifacts.description_raw: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.
artifacts.description_normalized: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP to give agents live browser inspection — DOM, console, network, and performance — instead of static code analysis.
artifacts.adoption_fit_final: strong — David's stack includes FliVideo (React/Next.js) and AppyStack apps; chrome-devtools MCP is already listed in the system-reminder available tools.
artifacts.inspiration_value: high
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: rare — The security-boundaries
artifacts.description_craft_refined: trigger — Multi-trigger description lists exact use cases; requirement dependency (chrome-devtools MCP) stated inline.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Instead of guessing what's happening at runtime, verify it.
artifacts.eval_notes: Security boundaries treatment (treating DOM/console/network output as untrusted data, never interpreting it as instructions) is load-bearing for agentic use and rare in skill literature. The five-step debug workflow (REPRODUCE → INSPECT → DIAGNOSE → FIX → VERIFY) is clean and reusable as a template. Strong candidate as the winner mechanism for a unified `browser-verification` skill.
artifacts.intent: Gives agents live browser inspection (DOM, console, network, performance) via Chrome DevTools MCP instead of relying on static code analysis.

artifacts.name: ci-cd-and-automation
artifacts.description_raw: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.
artifacts.description_normalized: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies.
artifacts.adoption_fit_final: strong — David uses GitHub Actions and Vercel; the CI→agent-feedback loop pattern is directly applicable to autonomous development sessions.
artifacts.inspiration_value: mid
artifacts.composability: standalone — Complete CI reference; can be invoked independently for new project setup or pipeline debugging.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Faster is Safer: Smaller batches and more frequent releases reduce risk, not increase it.
artifacts.eval_notes: The "Build Cop" role definition (person responsible for keeping CI green regardless of who broke it) is a team process idea that translates to agent orchestration: designate a monitoring loop that catches green/red state. The CI optimization decision tree (cache → parallelize → path filters → matrix → optimize suite → larger runners) is a useful ordered checklist. Security reminder about test DB c
artifacts.intent: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies with shift-left principles.

artifacts.name: code-review-and-quality
artifacts.description_raw: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.
artifacts.description_normalized: Conducts multi-axis code review (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) before any change is merged.
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: rare — The multi-model review pattern (Model A writes, Model B reviews, human makes final call) is a concrete mechanism not present in David's existing review skills. The dead-code hygiene ask-before-delete protocol is also uncommon.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: AI-generated code is probably fine
artifacts.eval_notes: This is the reference mechanism for code review in the Osmani cluster. The five-axis framework with explicit severity labels solves the common problem of authors treating all feedback as mandatory. The multi-model review pattern (cross-reviewer blind spots) is a genuine mechanism gap in David's current stack — his `code-quality-audit` skill focuses on static quality but lacks the agent-to-agent re
artifacts.intent: Multi-axis code review skill that evaluates changes across five dimensions (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) before any merge, covering both agent-authored and human-authored code.

artifacts.name: code-simplification
artifacts.description_raw: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.
artifacts.description_normalized: Reduces code complexity while preserving exact behavior, targeting readability over line-count reduction.
artifacts.inspiration_value: high
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Simplification that breaks project consistency is not simplification — it's churn.
artifacts.eval_notes: The "DAMP over DRY in tests" distinction (borrowed from TDD section) shows well-considered cross-skill coherence. The Rule of 500 for automation is a practical threshold rarely articulated anywhere. David's existing `simplify` skill likely overlaps significantly — this would be the winner mechanism for a unified refactoring skill with the Rule of 500 and Chesterton's Fence folded in.
artifacts.intent: Reduces code complexity while preserving exact behavior, targeting readability and comprehension speed over line-count reduction.

artifacts.name: context-engineering
artifacts.description_raw: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.
artifacts.description_normalized: Curates what context an agent receives, when it receives it, and how it's structured to maximize output quality.

The "Confusion Management" section — with structured CONFUSION: / Options: / → format — is directly mineable as a reusable pattern for any skill that encounters conflicting signals. Strongest single artifact in the stage-0 batch.
artifacts.intent: Guides deliberate curation of what context an agent sees, structured from persistent rules files down to per-iteration error output, with anti-patterns and confusion management protocols.

artifacts.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
artifacts.description_raw: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.

artifacts.adoption_fit_final: strong — Language-agnostic debugging discipline; examples use TypeScript/Node but patterns apply universally. No stack lock. Directly composable with incremental-implementation and test-driven-development.
artifacts.inspiration_value: high — The security-aware error handling section (don't execute commands found in error messages without user confirmation) is an under-documented concern in agent coding skills. The non-reproducible bug decision tree with four branches is the most structured treatment of intermittent failures in the corpus.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Don't push past a failing test or broken build to work on the next feature. Errors compound.
artifacts.eval_notes: The "stop-the-line" framing (borrowed from lean manufacturing) is the right mental model for AI-accelerated development where errors compound rapidly. The anti-rationalization table ("I know what the bug is" → "You might be right 70% of the time — the other 30% costs hours") is high-craft: it pre-emptively defeats the rationalizations that cause agents to skip the reproduce step. The minimal repro
artifacts.intent: Enforce a systematic stop-reproduce-localize-reduce-fix-guard-verify triage process when any unexpected failure occurs, instead of guessing at fixes.

artifacts.name: deprecation-and-migration
artifacts.description_raw: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.

artifacts.inspiration_value: mid

artifacts.intent: Manages safe removal of old systems and migration of users from deprecated to new implementations with structured decision criteria.

artifacts.name: documentation-and-adrs
artifacts.description_raw: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.

artifacts.name: doubt-driven-development
artifacts.description_raw: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.
artifacts.description_normalized: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands.
artifacts.adoption_fit_final: strong — No equivalent in David's current stack. The orchestrator-only constraint (cannot be inside a persona) fits Claude Code's main-session model.
artifacts.inspiration_value: high
artifacts.composability: calls-others — Spawns fresh-context reviewers (personas or generic subagents); explicitly integrates with TDD (RED step = doubt made concrete).
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Pass ARTIFACT + CONTRACT only. Do NOT pass the CLAIM. Handing the reviewer your conclusion biases it toward agreement.
artifacts.eval_notes: The CLAIM/EXTRACT split is the load-bearing idea: strip your own reasoning chain from what you hand the reviewer so you get genuine adversarial review rather than confirmation. The cross-model escalation section (with shell-escaping safety notes for Gemini/Codex) shows production-level thinking. The "doubt theater" red flag (two cycles, zero actionable findings = you are validating not doubting) i
artifacts.intent: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands, with cross-model escalation.

artifacts.intent: Enforce production-quality UI standards — design-system adherence, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), responsive design, and elimination of "AI aesthetic" patterns — when building or modifying user-facing interfaces.

artifacts.name: idea-refine

artifacts.name: incremental-implementation
artifacts.description_raw: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.
artifacts.description_normalized: Implements features in thin vertical slices — build one piece, test it, verify it, then expand — never writing large batches before testing.

The simplicity check table (EventBus vs simple function call; abstract factory vs two components; config-driven form builder vs three forms) is a concrete diagnostic tool rather than a vague quality directive. The red flag "To
artifacts.intent: Enforce thin vertical-slice delivery — implement one complete piece, test it, verify it, commit it — never writing more than ~100 lines before testing.

artifacts.name: interview-me
artifacts.description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

The before/after example showing how two questions revealed the actual ask was "a list" not "a dashboard" is a model for how to write a concrete example in any skill. The "Out of scope" line in the restate as non-ne
artifacts.intent: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want through one-question-at-a-time interviewing, each question paired with an explicit hypothesis, until 95% confidence is reached and a structured intent restate is confirmed.

artifacts.name: performance-optimization
artifacts.description_raw: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
artifacts.description_normalized: Measures then fixes performance bottlenecks — Core Web Vitals, load times, SLAs — never optimizing before profiling proves a problem exists.

The React.memo/useMemo red flag ("overusing is as bad as underusing") is a rare balanced statement in a field where most guidance only warns against one direction. The performance budget section (specific KB targets + CI enforcement co
artifacts.intent: Enforce measure-before-optimise discipline — establish baseline, identify specific bottleneck, fix it, re-measure, guard against regression — never optimising without profiling evidence.

artifacts.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
artifacts.description_raw: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.

artifacts.intent: Decomposes validated specs into verifiable tasks using dependency-graph ordering and vertical slicing (one complete feature path per task), with explicit sizing guidelines and human-review checkpoints.

artifacts.name: security-and-hardening

artifacts.intent: Apply security-first practices to every line of code touching user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations — treating all external input as hostile and all secrets as sacred.

artifacts.mineable_phrase: Friction > frequency. Pure repetition counts are weak signal. The same question asked twice is just normal work; a question that triggered a correction twice is a skill-shaped hole.
artifacts.eval_notes: The friction-over-frequency principle is directly applicable to David's stage 10 (observability gap identification): the places where the harness causes corrections, dead ends, or high cost are where new skills are needed — not places where tasks are simply repeated. artifacts.intent: Mines Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates via a friction × frequency × cost scoring model, with decay over time.

artifacts.name: kw-work
artifacts.description_raw: Execute a knowledge work plan. Break it into tasks, do the work, and track what happened. Use after planning to actually produce the deliverables.
artifacts.description_normalized: Loads a plan file, decomposes it into dependency-ordered task batches, executes each batch (parallelising where safe), and appends an execution log to the plan file for kw:compound to harvest.

artifacts.name: kw-compound
artifacts.description_raw: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.
artifacts.description_normalized: Scans session for 1-3 compoundable learnings (insight/playbook/correction/pattern), requires user approval, checks for duplicates and stale entries via stale-knowledge-checker agent, then writes YAML-frontmatter markdown to docs/knowledge/.
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: uncommon — Approval-required
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Nothing from this session seems worth saving as a standalone learning. The work is captured in the plan/deliverables.
artifacts.eval_notes: The explicit boundary — agents return text only, orchestrator writes files — is a clean separation of concerns worth preserving. Pipeline mode (skip confirmations, structured output) is a thoughtful addition for automation. The skill is narrow by design; its strength is the constraint "1-3 max" preventing knowledge bloat.
artifacts.intent: Closes a work session by extracting 1-3 approved learnings, deduplicating against existing knowledge, and saving structured markdown to `docs/knowledge/`.

artifacts.name: knowledge-base-researcher
artifacts.description_raw: Search docs/knowledge/ for saved learnings that are relevant to the current topic. Returns insights, corrections, playbooks, and patterns from past sessions. This is the agent that closes the compounding loop.
artifacts.description_normalized: Greps docs/knowledge/ by keyword and YAML tag to retrieve past learnings, grouping results as Directly Relevant / Tangentially Relevant / Not Found, and flags stale or low-confidence entries.

artifacts.name: past-work-researcher
artifacts.description_raw: Search plans/ and docs/solutions/ for related past work. Returns structured findings of what was decided, what data was used, and what worked. Use when /kw:plan needs to ground a new plan in prior context.
artifacts.description_normalized: Globs plans/ and docs/solutions/ for keyword matches, reads top 3-5 relevant files, and returns structured findings (decisions, data used, outcomes, unresolved questions) to ground new plans in prior context.

artifacts.name: stale-knowledge-checker
artifacts.description_raw: After a new learning is extracted, search docs/knowledge/ for entries that the new learning contradicts or supersedes. Returns candidates for update or removal. Prevents knowledge base from accumulating contradictions.
artifacts.description_normalized: Runs after kw:compound extracts a new learning; scans docs/knowledge/ for contradicted, superseded, or complementary entries and returns update/remove/keep recommendations without writing any files.
artifacts.adoption_fit_final: mid — Portable pattern; path convention is the only coupling; read-only contract makes it safe to embed anywhere
artifacts.inspiration_value: low — The contradiction/superseded/complementary taxonomy is sensible but not novel
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: uncommon — Read-only knowledge hygiene agent called before write is an underused pattern
artifacts.description_craft_refined: summary — Description explains the role clearly but lacks user-facing trigger phrases (it's an agent, not a user-invoked skill)
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Be conservative with 'remove' recommendations. Prefer 'update' or 'merge' over deletion. Knowledge is expensive to recreate.
artifacts.eval_notes: The hard constraint "return text only, never write files" is the key architectural decision — it keeps orchestration authority in the parent skill and prevents runaway deletions. The agent is appropriately narrow. Its value is entirely as a composable component; standalone it is too thin to justify adoption.
artifacts.intent: Sub-agent that searches `docs/knowledge/` for entries contradicted or superseded by a new learning, returning structured text recommendations without writing any files.

artifacts.description_normalized: Reviews plans and briefs for strategic soundness across 7 criteria (goal clarity, falsifiability, metrics, scope, resources, consistency, opportunity cost), returning P1/P2/P3 findings grouped by severity.

artifacts.name: agent-eval
artifacts.description_raw: Head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on custom tasks with pass rate, cost, time, and consistency metrics
artifacts.description_normalized: Head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on custom tasks with pass rate, cost, time, and consistency metrics
artifacts.inspiration_value: high — Frames agent selection as a data problem, not a vibes problem; the 3-trial consistency metric is an underused measurement idea
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: uncommon — Systematic multi-agent benchmarking on own codebase is rare; most teams use anecdote
artifacts.composability: standalone — Self-contained CLI; no upstream skill dependencies
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Every 'which coding agent is best?' comparison runs on vibes — this tool systematizes it.
artifacts.eval_notes: The worktree-per-run isolation pattern (no Docker, full reproducibility) is directly applicable to dark-factory evaluation pipelines. The instruction to track cost alongside pass rate ("95% agent at 10x cost") is a good prioritization heuristic. External repo dependency (github.com/joaquinhuigomez/agent-eval) means adoption requires vetting that repo first.
artifacts.intent: CLI tool for head-to-head comparison of coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, etc.) on YAML-defined tasks, measuring pass rate, cost, time, and consistency via git worktree isolation.

artifacts.name: agent-payment-x402
artifacts.description_raw: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents with per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets. Supports Base through agentwallet-sdk and X Layer through OKX Payments / OKX Agent Payments Protocol.
artifacts.description_normalized: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents with per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets.
artifacts.adoption_fit_final: weak — Tightly coupled to blockchain infrastructure (Base, X Layer, ERC-4337); only relevant if building agent-to-agent payment flows or paid API access
artifacts.inspiration_value: mid — The spending controls model (per-task budget
artifacts.uniqueness_refined: rare — Agent-native payment execution with policy-gated budgets is genuinely novel in the skills catalog space
artifacts.composability: standalone — MCP server integration; no skill dependencies, but requires external wallet infrastructure
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Set budgets before delegation: When spawning sub-agents, attach a SpendingPolicy via your orchestration layer. Never give an agent unlimited spend.
artifacts.eval_notes: The orchestrator-sets-policy-before-delegation pattern (not the agent itself) is the extractable governance idea for dark-factory: any high-privilege capability should be policy-bound at spawn time, not at execution time. The fail-closed preToolCheck implementation is production-grade. Adoption is niche — skip unless x402/crypto payments are on the roadmap.
artifacts.intent: Adds x402 HTTP payment execution to AI agents with per-task/session budget caps, allowlisted recipients, non-custodial ERC-4337 wallets, and MCP tool integration.

artifacts.name: briefing
artifacts.description_raw: Compile daily briefing with meeting context, active deals, and citation tracking.
artifacts.description_normalized: Compile daily briefing with meeting context, active deals, and citation tracking.

artifacts.name: citation-fixer
artifacts.description_raw: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages. Ensures every fact has an inline [Source: ...] citation matching the standard format. Extended in v0.25.1: scans for broken tweet/post references that lack actual URLs and resolves them via the host's X / Twitter API integration.
artifacts.description_normalized: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages.

artifacts.name: cold-start
artifacts.description_raw: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain. Sequences the highest-leverage data sources to go from empty brain to useful brain in one session. Uses ClawVisor for safe credential handling — the agent never holds raw API keys. Covers Gmail import, calendar sync, contacts seeding, X/Twitter archive, conversation imports, and file archives.
artifacts.description_normalized: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain.

artifacts.name: concept-synthesis
artifacts.description_raw: Deduplicate and synthesize raw concept stubs into a tiered intellectual map (T1 Canon to T4 Riff), tracing idea evolution across sources over time. Transforms thousands of raw concept pages into a curated intellectual fingerprint.
artifacts.description_normalized: Deduplicate and synthesize raw concept stubs into a tiered intellectual map (T1 Canon to T4 Riff), tracing idea evolution across sources over time.

artifacts.name: cron-scheduler
artifacts.description_raw: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override. Validates schedules, prevents collisions, and gates delivery during quiet hours.
artifacts.description_normalized: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override.

artifacts.name: cross-modal-review
artifacts.description_raw: Quality gate via second model. Spawn a different AI model to review work before committing. Includes refusal routing: if one model refuses, switch silently to the next. Extended in v0.25.1 with structured review-mode gating (when to invoke vs not) and a Codex code-review handoff for the diff-review case.
artifacts.description_normalized: Quality gate via second model.

artifacts.name: daily-task-manager
artifacts.description_raw: Task lifecycle management. Add, complete, defer, remove, and review tasks. Maintains a running task list as a brain page.
artifacts.description_normalized: Task lifecycle management.

artifacts.name: daily-task-prep
artifacts.description_raw: Morning preparation. Calendar lookahead, meeting context loading, open threads from yesterday, active task review. Extends briefing with actionable prep.
artifacts.description_normalized: Morning preparation.

artifacts.name: data-research
artifacts.description_raw: Structured data research: search sources, extract structured data, archive raw sources, maintain canonical tracker pages, deduplicate. Parameterized via YAML recipes for investor updates, donations, company updates, or any email-to-structured-data pipeline.
artifacts.description_normalized: Structured data research: search sources, extract structured data, archive raw sources, maintain canonical tracker pages, deduplicate.

artifacts.name: enrich
artifacts.description_raw: Enrich brain pages with tiered enrichment protocol. Creates and updates person/company pages with compiled truth, timeline, and cross-links. Use when a new entity is mentioned or an existing page needs updating.
artifacts.description_normalized: Enrich brain pages with tiered enrichment protocol.

artifacts.name: frontmatter-guard
artifacts.description_raw: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages. Catches malformed pages before they enter the brain (missing closing ---, nested quotes, slug mismatches, null bytes, empty frontmatter, YAML parse failures). Wraps the gbrain frontmatter CLI for agent-driven workflows.
artifacts.description_normalized: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages.

artifacts.name: functional-area-resolver
artifacts.description_raw: Compress an agent's routing file (RESOLVER.md or AGENTS.md) by converting granular skill-per-row tables into functional-area dispatchers. Each area lists sub-skills in a '(dispatcher for: ...)' clause. The LLM reads one area entry and routes to the correct sub-skill. Proven via held-out A/B eval: dispatcher pattern outperforms naive pipe-table compression.
artifacts.description_normalized: Compress an agent's routing file (RESOLVER.

artifacts.name: idea-ingest
artifacts.description_raw: Ingest links, articles, tweets, and ideas into the brain. Fetch content, save to brain with analysis, create author people page, and cross-link. Use when the user shares a link or says 'read this', 'save this', 'think about this'.
artifacts.description_normalized: Ingest links, articles, tweets, and ideas into the brain.

artifacts.name: ingest
artifacts.description_raw: Route content to specialized ingestion skills. Detects input type and delegates.
artifacts.description_normalized: Route content to specialized ingestion skills.

artifacts.name: install
artifacts.description_raw: Install GBrain (Deprecated). This skill has been replaced by the setup skill. See skills/setup/SKILL.md.
artifacts.description_normalized: Install GBrain (Deprecated).

artifacts.name: maintain
artifacts.description_raw: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks. Use when asked to check brain health, run maintenance, or audit quality.
artifacts.description_normalized: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks.

artifacts.name: media-ingest
artifacts.description_raw: Ingest video, audio, PDF, book, screenshot, and GitHub repo content into the brain. Multi-format handling with entity extraction and backlink propagation. Covers video-ingest, youtube-ingest, and book-ingest subtypes.
artifacts.description_normalized: Ingest video, audio, PDF, book, screenshot, and GitHub repo content into the brain.

artifacts.name: meeting-ingestion
artifacts.description_raw: Ingest meeting transcripts into brain pages with attendee enrichment, entity propagation, and timeline merge. A meeting is NOT fully ingested until the enrich skill has processed every entity.
artifacts.description_normalized: Ingest meeting transcripts into brain pages with attendee enrichment, entity propagation, and timeline merge.

artifacts.name: migrate
artifacts.description_raw: Universal migration from Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam.
artifacts.description_normalized: Universal migration from Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam.

artifacts.name: minion-orchestrator
artifacts.description_raw: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration. Replaces the older gbrain-jobs routing intent. Use when: submitting gbrain jobs, shell/background tasks, spawning subagents, checking progress, steering running work, pausing/resuming, parallel fan-out. One durable, observable, steerable queue interface.
artifacts.description_normalized: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration.

artifacts.name: perplexity-research
artifacts.description_raw: Brain-augmented web research. Sends brain context about a topic to Perplexity, which searches the web with citations and returns what is NEW vs what the brain already knows. Use for entity enrichment, current-state checks, deal monitoring, and freshness deltas. NOT for simple URL fetches (use web_fetch) or brain-only queries (use gbrain query).
artifacts.description_normalized: Brain-augmented web research.

artifacts.name: publish
artifacts.description_raw: Share brain pages as beautiful password-protected HTML with zero LLM calls.
artifacts.description_normalized: Share brain pages as beautiful password-protected HTML with zero LLM calls.

artifacts.name: query
artifacts.description_raw: Answer questions using the brain's knowledge with 3-layer search, synthesis, and citation propagation. Use when the user asks a question, wants a lookup, or needs information from the brain.
artifacts.description_normalized: Answer questions using the brain's knowledge with 3-layer search, synthesis, and citation propagation.

artifacts.name: repo-architecture
artifacts.description_raw: Where new brain files go. Decision protocol for filing brain pages by primary subject, not by format or source. Reference for all brain-writing skills.
artifacts.description_normalized: Where new brain files go.

artifacts.name: reports
artifacts.description_raw: Save and load timestamped reports. Keyword routing for fast lookup. Cron jobs save output as reports; the agent or user queries them by keyword.
artifacts.description_normalized: Save and load timestamped reports.

artifacts.name: setup
artifacts.description_raw: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.md injection, first import.
artifacts.description_normalized: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.

artifacts.name: signal-detector
artifacts.description_raw: Always-on ambient signal capture. Fires on every inbound message to detect original thinking and entity mentions. Spawn as a cheap sub-agent in parallel, never block the main response.
artifacts.description_normalized: Always-on ambient signal capture.

artifacts.name: skill-creator
artifacts.description_raw: Create new skills following the GBrain conformance standard. Generates SKILL.md with frontmatter, Contract, Phases, Output Format, and Anti-Patterns. Checks MECE against existing skills. Updates manifest and resolver.
artifacts.description_normalized: Create new skills following the GBrain conformance standard.

artifacts.name: skillify
artifacts.description_raw: The meta skill. Turn any raw feature into a properly-skilled, tested, resolvable unit of agent capability. Cross-modal eval is the recommended Phase 3 quality gate: 3 frontier models from different providers critique the output, you iterate to quality, THEN write tests that lock in the proven-good behavior.
artifacts.description_normalized: The meta skill.

artifacts.name: skillpack-check
artifacts.description_raw: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install. Wraps gbrain doctor + gbrain apply-migrations --list so a host agent can see at a glance whether the skillpack needs attention. Use when the user asks 'is gbrain healthy?', when a cron fires a morning check, or proactively when something seems off.
artifacts.description_normalized: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install.

artifacts.name: smoke-test
artifacts.description_raw: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments. Tests critical services, auto-fixes known issues, extensible via user-defined test scripts in ~/.gbrain/smoke-tests.d/*.sh.
artifacts.description_normalized: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments.

artifacts.name: soul-audit
artifacts.description_raw: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.md), user profile (USER.md), access control (ACCESS_POLICY.md), and operational cadence (HEARTBEAT.md). Re-runnable anytime to update any section.
artifacts.description_normalized: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.

artifacts.name: strategic-reading
artifacts.description_raw: Read a book, article, transcript, or case study through the lens of a specific strategic problem you're facing. Produces an applied playbook that maps the source onto the problem and gives short/medium/long-term recommendations. NOT for general book summaries.
artifacts.description_normalized: Read a book, article, transcript, or case study through the lens of a specific strategic problem you're facing.

artifacts.name: testing
artifacts.description_raw: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence. Validates skill conformance (frontmatter, manifest coverage, resolver coverage). Runs the project test suite in tiered phases (unit / evals / integration / system health), classifies failures, and produces a regression-aware report.
artifacts.description_normalized: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence.

artifacts.name: voice-note-ingest
artifacts.description_raw: Ingest a voice note with exact-phrasing preservation (never paraphrased). Routes content to originals/, concepts/, people/, companies/, ideas/, personal/, or voice-notes/ based on a decision tree. The user's exact words are the signal.
artifacts.description_normalized: Ingest a voice note with exact-phrasing preservation (never paraphrased).

artifacts.name: webhook-transforms
artifacts.description_raw: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals. Define a transform function, register a webhook URL, and incoming events get processed through the brain pipeline.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals.

artifacts.name: calendar-to-brain
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.

artifacts.name: credential-gateway
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.

artifacts.name: email-to-brain
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.

artifacts.name: meeting-sync
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.

artifacts.name: ngrok-tunnel
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.

artifacts.name: restart-sweep
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.

artifacts.name: twilio-voice-brain
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.

artifacts.name: x-to-brain
artifacts.description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.
artifacts.description_normalized: Integration recipe: Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.

artifacts.name: add-backlog
artifacts.description_raw: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering). Add a backlog item to the roadmap using 999.x numbering. Backlog items are unsequenced ideas that aren't ready for active planning — they live outside the normal phase sequence and accumulate context over time.
artifacts.description_normalized: Adds an unsequenced idea to the roadmap backlog using 999.x phase numbering.

artifacts.name: add-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap. Handles: phase number calculation (next sequential integer), directory creation with slug generation, roadmap structure updates, STATE.md roadmap evolution tracking.
artifacts.description_normalized: Appends a new sequential integer phase to the end of the current milestone roadmap.

artifacts.name: add-tests
artifacts.description_raw: Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation. Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase, using its SUMMARY.md, CONTEXT.md, and VERIFICATION.md as specifications.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase using its planning artifacts as specifications.

artifacts.name: add-todo
artifacts.description_raw: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context. Handles: directory structure creation, content extraction from arguments or conversation, area inference from file paths, duplicate detection and resolution, todo file creation with frontmatter.
artifacts.description_normalized: Captures an idea or task surfaced in a GSD session as a structured todo for later work.

artifacts.name: analyze-dependencies
artifacts.description_raw: Analyze phase dependencies and suggest Depends on entries for ROADMAP.md. Analyzes the phase dependency graph for the current milestone: file overlap, semantic dependencies, data flow.
artifacts.description_normalized: Analyzes the phase dependency graph and suggests ROADMAP.md Depends-on entries.

artifacts.name: audit-milestone
artifacts.description_raw: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving. Verifies milestone achieved its definition of done. Reads existing VERIFICATION.md files, aggregates tech debt and deferred gaps, then spawns integration checker for cross-phase wiring.
artifacts.description_normalized: Audits milestone completion against requirements before archiving, spawning an integration checker for cross-phase verification.

artifacts.name: audit-uat
artifacts.description_raw: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items. Scans all phases for pending, skipped, blocked, and human_needed UAT items. Cross-references against codebase to detect stale documentation.
artifacts.description_normalized: Scans all phases for outstanding UAT items and cross-references against the live codebase to detect stale documentation.

artifacts.name: autonomous
artifacts.description_raw: Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase. Pauses only for user decisions (grey area acceptance, blockers, validation requests). Uses ROADMAP.md phase discovery and Skill() flat invocations for each phase command.
artifacts.description_normalized: Runs all remaining milestone phases autonomously (discuss→plan→execute), pausing only for user decisions.

artifacts.name: check-todos
artifacts.description_raw: List pending todos and select one to work on. Handles: todo counting and listing with area filtering, interactive selection with full context loading, roadmap correlation checking, action routing (work now, add to phase, brainstorm, create phase), STATE.md updates.
artifacts.description_normalized: Lists pending todos, allows selection, and routes to the appropriate action (work now, promote to phase, etc.).

artifacts.name: cleanup
artifacts.description_raw: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones into .planning/milestones/v{X.Y}-phases/. Use when .planning/phases/ has accumulated directories from past milestones.
artifacts.description_normalized: Archives completed milestone phase directories into a versioned archive folder.

artifacts.name: complete-milestone
artifacts.description_raw: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version. Mark milestone complete, archive to milestones/, update ROADMAP.md and REQUIREMENTS.md. Creates historical record of shipped version.
artifacts.description_normalized: Archives a completed milestone, updates planning files, and git-tags the historical record.

artifacts.name: debug
artifacts.description_raw: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets. Orchestrator gathers symptoms, spawns gsd-debugger agent, handles checkpoints, spawns continuations. --diagnose flag diagnoses only without fix.
artifacts.description_normalized: Orchestrates systematic debugging by spawning a fresh-context gsd-debugger subagent with persistent state across context resets.

artifacts.name: discuss-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning. Extracts implementation decisions that downstream agents need. --auto skips interactive questions, --chain chains to plan+execute, --power generates questions into file-based UI.
artifacts.description_normalized: Gathers phase implementation decisions through adaptive questioning, producing CONTEXT.md consumed by researcher and planner.

artifacts.name: do
artifacts.description_raw: Route freeform text to the right GSD command automatically. Analyzes freeform natural language input and dispatches to the most appropriate GSD command. Never does the work itself.
artifacts.description_normalized: Dispatches freeform natural language to the appropriate GSD slash command via intent routing.

artifacts.name: docs-update
artifacts.description_raw: Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase. Each doc type is written by a gsd-doc-writer subagent that explores the codebase directly — no hallucinated paths, phantom endpoints, or stale signatures.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates up to 9 verified project documentation files using parallel gsd-doc-writer subagents that explore the codebase directly.

artifacts.name: execute-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization. Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context.
artifacts.description_normalized: Executes all phase plans in dependency-ordered parallel waves using fresh-context gsd-executor subagents.

artifacts.name: fast
artifacts.description_raw: Execute a trivial task inline — no subagents, no planning overhead. For tasks too small to justify planning overhead: typo fixes, config changes, small refactors, forgotten commits, simple additions.
artifacts.description_normalized: Executes trivial tasks directly in the current context without subagents or planning overhead.

artifacts.name: forensics
artifacts.description_raw: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong. Produces forensic report saved to .planning/forensics/.
artifacts.description_normalized: Post-mortem investigation of failed GSD workflows by analyzing git history, planning artifacts, and filesystem state.

artifacts.name: health
artifacts.description_raw: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues. Validates .planning/ directory integrity and reports actionable issues: missing files, invalid configurations, inconsistent state, and orphaned plans.
artifacts.description_normalized: Diagnoses .planning/ directory integrity and optionally repairs issues like missing files and inconsistent state.

artifacts.name: help
artifacts.description_raw: Show available GSD commands and usage guide. Output ONLY the reference content. Do NOT add project-specific analysis, git status, next-step suggestions, or any commentary beyond the reference.
artifacts.description_normalized: Displays the complete GSD command reference with strict no-commentary output constraint.

artifacts.name: insert-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases. Handle urgent work discovered during execution without renumbering entire roadmap.
artifacts.description_normalized: Inserts a decimal-numbered urgent phase between existing integer phases without renumbering the roadmap.

artifacts.name: join-discord
artifacts.description_raw: Join the GSD Discord community. Displays the Discord invite link for the GSD community server.
artifacts.description_normalized: Displays the GSD Discord community invite link.

artifacts.name: list-phase-assumptions
artifacts.description_raw: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning. Analyzes a phase and presents assumptions about technical approach, implementation order, scope boundaries, risk areas, and dependencies.
artifacts.description_normalized: Surfaces the agent's implicit assumptions about a phase before planning begins, enabling early course correction.

artifacts.name: list-workspaces
artifacts.description_raw: List all GSD workspaces with status overview.
artifacts.description_normalized: Lists all GSD workspaces with their current status overview.

artifacts.name: manager
artifacts.description_raw: Single-terminal command center for managing a milestone. Shows a dashboard of all phases with visual status indicators, recommends optimal next actions, and dispatches work — discuss runs inline, plan/execute run as background agents.
artifacts.description_normalized: Single-terminal milestone command center with phase dashboard and intelligent work dispatching.

artifacts.name: map-codebase
artifacts.description_raw: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents. Uses parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents for tech, arch, quality, and concerns focus areas.
artifacts.description_normalized: Analyzes the codebase using parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents across four focus dimensions, writing structured analysis documents.

artifacts.name: milestone-summary
artifacts.description_raw: Generate a comprehensive project summary from milestone artifacts for team onboarding and review. Reads completed milestone artifacts and produces a human-friendly overview of what was built, how, and why.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates a human-friendly milestone summary from planning artifacts for team onboarding and project review.

artifacts.name: new-milestone
artifacts.description_raw: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements. Brownfield equivalent of new-project. Gathers 'what's next', updates PROJECT.md, then runs requirements → roadmap cycle.
artifacts.description_normalized: Starts a new milestone cycle on an existing project by gathering intent and routing to requirements and roadmap creation.

artifacts.name: new-project
artifacts.description_raw: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md. Includes a Copilot runtime note for VS Code compatibility (vscode_askquestions equivalent to AskUserQuestion).
artifacts.description_normalized: Initializes a new project with deep context gathering, requirements extraction, and PROJECT.md creation.

artifacts.name: new-workspace
artifacts.description_raw: Create an isolated workspace with repo copies and independent .planning/. Supports --strategy worktree|clone with --name, --repos, --path, --branch, --auto flags.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates an isolated GSD workspace with independent .planning/ via worktree or clone strategy.

artifacts.name: next
artifacts.description_raw: Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GSD workflow. Reads STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, and phase directories to determine what comes next. Designed for rapid multi-project workflows.
artifacts.description_normalized: Detects current project state and auto-advances to the next logical GSD workflow step.

artifacts.name: note
artifacts.description_raw: Zero-friction idea capture. Append, list, or promote notes to todos. Three subcommands: append (default), list, promote.
artifacts.description_normalized: Zero-friction timestamped note capture with list and promote-to-todo subcommands.

artifacts.name: pause-work
artifacts.description_raw: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase. Creates .continue-here.md handoff file to preserve complete work state across sessions.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates a .continue-here.md context handoff file to preserve work state across session pauses.

artifacts.name: plan-milestone-gaps
artifacts.description_raw: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit. Reads MILESTONE-AUDIT.md, groups gaps into logical phases, creates phase entries in ROADMAP.md, and offers to plan each phase.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates ROADMAP phases to close all gaps identified by /gsd-audit-milestone.

artifacts.name: plan-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop. Orchestrates: 4 parallel phase-researcher agents → research synthesizer → planner → plan-checker (loop ≤3). Supports --auto, --research, --skip-research, --gaps, --skip-verify, --prd, --reviews, --text flags.
artifacts.description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel research, planning, and verification to produce a PLAN.md for a phase.

artifacts.name: plant-seed
artifacts.description_raw: Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions — surfaces automatically at the right milestone. Seeds solve context rot: instead of a one-liner in Deferred that nobody acts on, seeds carry full context and trigger conditions.
artifacts.description_normalized: Captures a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions that surfaces automatically at the right milestone.

artifacts.name: pr-branch
artifacts.description_raw: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out .planning/ commits — ready for code review. Solves the problem of PR diffs being cluttered with PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, STATE.md artifacts.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates a clean PR branch by cherry-picking commits that exclude .planning/ artifacts.

artifacts.name: profile-user
artifacts.description_raw: Generate developer behavioral profile and create Claude-discoverable artifacts. Analyzes session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions via spawned gsd-user-profiler agent.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates a Claude-discoverable developer behavioral profile by spawning gsd-user-profiler to analyze session messages.

artifacts.name: progress
artifacts.description_raw: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan). Provides situational awareness before continuing work.
artifacts.description_normalized: Shows project progress summary and routes to the next action (execute existing plan or create a new one).

artifacts.name: quick
artifacts.description_raw: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents. Flags: --full (enable all agents), --validate (add verification), --discuss (enable discussion), --research (enable research).
artifacts.description_normalized: Executes a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) while skipping optional agent overhead.

artifacts.name: reapply-patches
artifacts.description_raw: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update. Uses three-way comparison (pristine baseline, user-modified backup, newly installed version) to reliably distinguish user customizations from version drift. Critical invariant: never conclude 'no custom content' for any backed-up file.
artifacts.description_normalized: Reapplies user customizations after a GSD update using three-way comparison to distinguish custom content from version drift.

artifacts.name: remove-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases. Clean removal of work you've decided not to do, without polluting context with cancelled/deferred markers.
artifacts.description_normalized: Removes an unstarted phase from the roadmap and renumbers subsequent phases cleanly.

artifacts.name: remove-workspace
artifacts.description_raw: Remove a GSD workspace and clean up worktrees.
artifacts.description_normalized: Removes a GSD workspace and cleans up associated git worktrees.

artifacts.name: research-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Research how to implement a phase (standalone — usually use /gsd-plan-phase instead). Spawns gsd-phase-researcher agent with phase context. Note: standalone; plan-phase integrates research automatically.
artifacts.description_normalized: Standalone command that spawns gsd-phase-researcher to research phase implementation (normally invoked by plan-phase).

artifacts.name: resume-work
artifacts.description_raw: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration. Handles: .continue-here.md detection, STATE.md context loading, current phase identification, SlashCommand routing to appropriate next step.
artifacts.description_normalized: Restores full project context from previous session and routes to the appropriate next workflow step.

artifacts.name: review-backlog
artifacts.description_raw: Review and promote backlog items to active milestone. Reviews all 999.x backlog items and optionally promotes them into the active milestone sequence or removes stale entries.
artifacts.description_normalized: Reviews all 999.x backlog items and promotes selected ones into the active milestone or removes stale entries.

artifacts.name: review
artifacts.description_raw: Request cross-AI peer review of phase plans from external AI CLIs (Gemini, Claude, Codex, OpenCode). Produces a structured REVIEWS.md with per-reviewer feedback.
artifacts.description_normalized: Invokes external AI CLIs (Gemini, Claude, Codex, OpenCode) to independently peer-review phase plans.

artifacts.name: secure-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase. Spawns gsd-security-auditor to verify that threat mitigations declared in PLAN.md are present in implemented code.
artifacts.description_normalized: Retroactively verifies that phase threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in the implemented codebase.

artifacts.name: session-report
artifacts.description_raw: Generate a session report with token usage estimates, work summary, and outcomes. Produces SESSION_REPORT.md document capturing session outcomes, work performed, and estimated resource usage.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates a SESSION_REPORT.md capturing session outcomes, work performed, and token usage estimates.

artifacts.name: set-profile
artifacts.description_raw: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget/inherit). Calls gsd-tools.cjs config-set-model-profile directly via bash.
artifacts.description_normalized: Switches the model profile for all GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget/inherit) via gsd-tools config.

artifacts.name: settings
artifacts.description_raw: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile. Interactive configuration of GSD workflow agents and model profile via multi-question prompt.
artifacts.description_normalized: Interactive multi-question configuration of GSD workflow agent toggles and model profile.

artifacts.name: ship
artifacts.description_raw: Create PR, run review, and prepare for merge after verification passes. Push branch, create PR with auto-generated body, optionally trigger review, and track the merge.
artifacts.description_normalized: Bridges local completion to merged PR by pushing, creating a PR with auto-generated body, and tracking the merge.

artifacts.name: stats
artifacts.description_raw: Display project statistics — phases, plans, requirements, git metrics, and timeline. Comprehensive project statistics including phase progress, plan execution metrics, requirements completion, git history stats.
artifacts.description_normalized: Displays comprehensive project statistics: phases, plans, requirements, git metrics, and timeline.

artifacts.name: thread
artifacts.description_raw: Manage persistent context threads for cross-session work. Threads are lightweight cross-session knowledge stores for work that spans multiple sessions but doesn't belong to any specific phase.
artifacts.description_normalized: Manages persistent cross-session context threads for work that spans sessions but doesn't belong to a specific phase.

artifacts.name: ui-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Generate UI design contract (UI-SPEC.md) for frontend phases. Spawns gsd-ui-researcher then gsd-ui-checker in a verification loop.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates a UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases using researcher and checker subagents.

artifacts.name: ui-review
artifacts.description_raw: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Spawns gsd-ui-auditor to produce scored UI-REVIEW.md.
artifacts.description_normalized: Conducts a retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code via gsd-ui-auditor.

artifacts.name: update
artifacts.description_raw: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display. Handles: version detection (local vs global installation), npm version checking, changelog fetching and display.
artifacts.description_normalized: Updates GSD to the latest npm version and displays the changelog.

artifacts.name: validate-phase
artifacts.description_raw: Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase. Spawns gsd-nyquist-auditor to generate and run tests for each gap.
artifacts.description_normalized: Retroactively audits and fills Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase using gsd-nyquist-auditor.

artifacts.name: verify-work
artifacts.description_raw: Validate built features through conversational UAT. Goal-backward analysis: start from what the phase SHOULD deliver, verify it actually exists and works. Spawns gsd-verifier.
artifacts.description_normalized: Validates built features through conversational UAT using goal-backward analysis via gsd-verifier.

artifacts.name: workstreams
artifacts.description_raw: Manage parallel workstreams — list, create, switch, status, progress, complete, and resume. Supports subcommands for concurrent milestone work.
artifacts.description_normalized: Manages parallel workstreams for concurrent milestone work with subcommands for list/create/switch/status/complete/resume.

artifacts.name: gsd-advisor-researcher
artifacts.description_raw: Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode. Returns structured output for the main agent to synthesize — does NOT present to user directly.
artifacts.description_normalized: Researches a single gray-area decision and returns a structured 5-column comparison table for discuss-phase synthesis.

artifacts.name: gsd-assumptions-analyzer
artifacts.description_raw: Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode. Searches codebase for files related to the phase, produces assumptions with file path evidence and confidence levels.
artifacts.description_normalized: Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with file-path evidence and confidence levels.

artifacts.name: gsd-codebase-mapper
artifacts.description_raw: Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.
artifacts.description_normalized: Explores a codebase focus area (tech/arch/quality/concerns) and writes structured analysis documents to .planning/codebase/.

artifacts.name: gsd-debugger
artifacts.description_raw: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd-debug orchestrator. Manages persistent debug sessions across context resets.
artifacts.description_normalized: Investigates bugs via scientific method, managing persistent debug sessions with checkpoints across context resets.

artifacts.name: gsd-doc-verifier
artifacts.description_raw: Verifies factual claims in generated docs against the live codebase. Returns structured JSON per doc. Spawned by /gsd-docs-update workflow.
artifacts.description_normalized: Verifies factual claims in generated documentation against the live codebase, returning structured JSON per doc.

artifacts.name: gsd-doc-writer
artifacts.description_raw: Writes and updates project documentation. Spawned with a doc_assignment block specifying doc type, mode (create/update/supplement/fix), and project context. Supports 10 doc types.
artifacts.description_normalized: Writes and updates project documentation files across 10 doc types in create/update/supplement/fix modes.

artifacts.name: gsd-executor
artifacts.description_raw: Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command. Each executor gets fresh 200K context.
artifacts.description_normalized: Executes PLAN.md files atomically with per-task commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and SUMMARY.md creation.

artifacts.name: gsd-integration-checker
artifacts.description_raw: Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end. Principle: Existence != Integration.
artifacts.description_normalized: Verifies cross-phase wiring and E2E user flows, applying the 'existence does not equal integration' principle.

artifacts.name: gsd-nyquist-auditor
artifacts.description_raw: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements. Spawned by /gsd-validate-phase. For each gap: generate minimal behavioral test, run it, debug if failing (max 3 iterations).
artifacts.description_normalized: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating and running minimal behavioral tests for each uncovered phase requirement.

artifacts.name: gsd-phase-researcher
artifacts.description_raw: Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. Answers 'What do I need to know to PLAN this phase well?'
artifacts.description_normalized: Researches phase implementation and produces RESEARCH.md answering 'what do I need to know to plan this phase well?'

artifacts.name: gsd-plan-checker
artifacts.description_raw: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. Critical mindset: start from what the phase SHOULD deliver, verify plans address it.
artifacts.description_normalized: Verifies that plans will actually achieve the phase goal via goal-backward analysis before execution.

artifacts.name: gsd-planner
artifacts.description_raw: Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator in standard, gaps, or revision mode.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates executable PLAN.md files with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification.

artifacts.name: gsd-project-researcher
artifacts.description_raw: Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project or /gsd-new-milestone orchestrators. Answers 'What does this domain ecosystem look like?'
artifacts.description_normalized: Researches the domain ecosystem before roadmap creation, producing research files in .planning/research/.

artifacts.name: gsd-research-synthesizer
artifacts.description_raw: Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd-new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.
artifacts.description_normalized: Synthesizes outputs from 4 parallel researcher agents into a cohesive RESEARCH-SUMMARY.md.

artifacts.name: gsd-roadmapper
artifacts.description_raw: Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project orchestrator.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates project roadmaps mapping requirements to phases with goal-backward success criteria and coverage validation.

artifacts.name: gsd-security-auditor

artifacts.description_normalized: Verifies that declared threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in implemented code; does not scan for new vulnerabilities.

artifacts.description_raw: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd-ui-review orchestrator.
artifacts.description_normalized: Conducts a retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code and produces a scored UI-REVIEW.md.

artifacts.description_normalized: Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent

artifacts.name: constitution

artifacts.name: specify
artifacts.description_raw: Create or update the feature specification from a natural language feature description.
artifacts.description_normalized: Converts a natural-language feature description into a structured spec.md artifact.

artifacts.name: clarify
artifacts.description_raw: Identify underspecified areas in the current feature spec by asking up to 5 highly targeted clarification questions and encoding answers back into the spec.
artifacts.description_normalized: Surfaces up to 5 clarification questions for an existing spec and encodes the answers back into spec.md.

artifacts.name: plan
artifacts.description_raw: Execute the implementation planning workflow using the plan template to generate design artifacts.
artifacts.description_normalized: Generates a plan.md from the feature spec using the plan template and produces design artifacts.

artifacts.name: tasks
artifacts.description_raw: Generate an actionable, dependency-ordered tasks.md for the feature based on available design artifacts.
artifacts.description_normalized: Produces a dependency-ordered tasks.md from the plan and spec artifacts.

artifacts.name: checklist
artifacts.description_raw: Generate a custom checklist for the current feature based on user requirements.
artifacts.description_normalized: Creates a requirements-quality checklist — unit tests for the spec's completeness and clarity, not for implementation verification.

artifacts.name: analyze
artifacts.description_raw: Perform a non-destructive cross-artifact consistency and quality analysis across spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md after task generation.
artifacts.description_normalized: Read-only consistency gate that cross-checks spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md before implementation is allowed to proceed.

artifacts.name: implement
artifacts.description_raw: Execute the implementation plan by processing and executing all tasks defined in tasks.md
artifacts.description_normalized: Drives code implementation by sequentially executing each task from tasks.md.

artifacts.name: taskstoissues
artifacts.description_raw: Convert existing tasks into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub Issues for the feature based on available design artifacts.
artifacts.description_normalized: Converts tasks.md entries into GitHub Issues via MCP, preserving dependency ordering.

artifacts.description_normalized: Git extension command that creates a numbered feature branch before the specify phase begins.

artifacts.name: speckit.git.commit
artifacts.description_raw: Auto-commit changes after a Spec Kit command completes
artifacts.description_normalized: Git extension command that auto-stages and commits all changes after any Spec Kit phase completes.

artifacts.name: speckit.git.initialize
artifacts.description_raw: Initialize a Git repository with an initial commit
artifacts.description_normalized: Git extension command that initializes a Git repo with an initial commit if one does not already exist.

artifacts.name: speckit.git.remote
artifacts.description_raw: Detect Git remote URL for GitHub integration
artifacts.description_normalized: Git extension command that detects the remote URL for GitHub MCP integration during taskstoissues.

artifacts.name: speckit.git.validate

artifacts.description_normalized: Git extension command that validates the active branch conforms to spec-kit feature branch naming rules.

artifacts.name: lean.plan
artifacts.description_raw: Create a plan and store it in plan.md.
artifacts.description_normalized: Lean preset override of plan — minimal planning variant for teams who skip clarify and checklist phases.

artifacts.name: lean.tasks
artifacts.description_raw: Create the tasks needed for implementation and store them in tasks.md.
artifacts.description_normalized: Lean preset override of tasks — minimal task-generation variant without analyze gate.

artifacts.name: lean.implement
