artifacts.description_normalized: Lean preset override of implement — stripped variant that processes tasks.md without optional extension hooks.

artifacts.name: scaffold.specify
artifacts.description_raw: Create a feature specification (preset override)
artifacts.description_normalized: Scaffold preset override of specify — demonstrates how to create a custom specify variant when building a new preset.

artifacts.name: self-test.specify
artifacts.description_raw: Self-test override of the specify command
artifacts.description_normalized: Self-test preset override of specify — used to validate the lifecycle of the specify phase during automated preset testing.

artifacts.name: self-test.wrap-test
artifacts.description_raw: Self-test wrap command — pre/post around core
artifacts.description_normalized: Self-test preset wrap command that injects pre/post logic around a core command template using the wrap strategy.

artifacts.name: brainstorming
artifacts.description_raw: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation. Guides a structured spec-and-design dialogue: one question at a time, 2-3 approach proposals, design approval gate, write design doc, spec self-review, then hand off to writing-plans.
artifacts.description_normalized: Structured design dialogue skill that turns ideas into approved spec documents before any implementation begins.

artifacts.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
artifacts.description_raw: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies. Delegates each independent problem domain to a focused subagent with precisely crafted context, runs them concurrently, then reviews and integrates the results.
artifacts.description_normalized: Parallel subagent dispatch skill for independent tasks that can run concurrently without shared state.

artifacts.name: executing-plans
artifacts.description_raw: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints. Loads plan, reviews critically, executes all tasks following each step exactly, stops and asks on blockers, then hands off to finishing-a-development-branch.
artifacts.description_normalized: Sequential plan execution skill for running a written implementation plan task-by-task with blocking checkpoints.

artifacts.name: finishing-a-development-branch
artifacts.description_raw: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup. Verifies tests, detects environment (worktree vs normal repo, detached HEAD), presents exactly 4 or 3 structured options, executes choice, cleans up workspace with provenance check.
artifacts.description_normalized: Branch completion skill that verifies tests and presents structured merge/PR/keep/discard options with safe cleanup.

artifacts.name: receiving-code-review
artifacts.description_raw: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation. Enforces: read, understand, verify, evaluate, respond technically, implement one item at a time.
artifacts.description_normalized: Code review reception skill that enforces technical verification over performative agreement before implementing any feedback.

artifacts.name: requesting-code-review
artifacts.description_raw: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements. Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with precisely crafted context (git SHAs, description, requirements) to catch issues before they cascade. Mandatory after each task in subagent-driven development.
artifacts.description_normalized: Code review request skill that dispatches a focused reviewer subagent with crafted context after each implementation task.

artifacts.name: subagent-driven-development
artifacts.description_raw: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session. Dispatches fresh implementer subagent per task (receives full task text, never reads plan file), followed by two-stage review: spec compliance reviewer then code quality reviewer. Continuous execution — no check-ins between tasks. Ends with final code review and finishing-a-development-branch.
artifacts.description_normalized: Continuous plan execution skill using fresh subagents per task with mandatory two-stage spec-then-quality review gates.

artifacts.name: systematic-debugging
artifacts.description_raw: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Four phases: root cause investigation (gather evidence), pattern analysis (find working examples), hypothesis and testing (one variable at a time), implementation (failing test first, then fix). After 3+ failed fixes, question the architecture.
artifacts.description_normalized: Systematic root-cause debugging skill that enforces evidence-first investigation through four mandatory phases before any fix.

artifacts.name: test-driven-development
artifacts.description_raw: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code. Iron Law: NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Red-Green-Refactor cycle: write failing test, verify it fails for the right reason, write minimal code to pass, verify passes, refactor. No exceptions — delete code written before tests and start over. Includes Rationalizations table of verbatim agent excuses.
artifacts.description_normalized: Strict TDD skill enforcing test-first development with Iron Law, Red-Green-Refactor cycle, and pre-emptive rationalization counters.

artifacts.name: using-git-worktrees
artifacts.description_raw: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback. Detects existing isolation first, defers to native harness tools, falls back to manual git worktree with safety verification, then runs project setup and clean baseline test.
artifacts.description_normalized: Workspace isolation skill that detects existing worktrees, defers to native harness tools, and falls back to git worktrees with safety checks.

artifacts.name: using-superpowers
artifacts.description_raw: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions. Even 1% chance a skill applies = must invoke it. Includes Red Flags rationalization table, skill priority order, and SUBAGENT-STOP guard. This skill body is injected at session start via hooks/session-start bootstrap.
artifacts.description_normalized: Session bootstrap skill that enforces mandatory skill invocation before any action, with anti-rationalization guards.

artifacts.name: verification-before-completion
artifacts.description_raw: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always. Iron Law: NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE. Gate function: identify command, run it fresh, read full output, verify, then claim. Includes Rationaliza
artifacts.description_normalized: Verification gate skill that requires fresh command evidence before any completion claim, commit, or PR creation.

artifacts.name: writing-plans
artifacts.description_raw: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code. Writes comprehensive implementation plans: file structure map first, then bite-sized tasks (2-5 min steps) with exact file paths, complete code, exact commands and expected output. No placeholders. Every plan includes required REQUIRED SUB-SKILL header. Ends with execution handoff choice (subagent-driven vs inlin
artifacts.description_normalized: Implementation plan authoring skill that produces zero-placeholder, TDD-structured, bite-sized task plans with complete code at each step.

artifacts.name: writing-skills
artifacts.description_raw: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment. Applies TDD to skill authoring: RED (run baseline pressure scenario, document verbatim rationalizations), GREEN (write minimal SKILL.md addressing those specific failures), REFACTOR (close loopholes, rebuild rationalization table, re-test). Iron Law: NO SKILL WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Includes Cl
artifacts.description_normalized: Skill authoring meta-skill applying TDD Red-Green-Refactor to documentation: baseline pressure testing before writing, rationalization tables, CSO-compliant descriptions.
